name: Kashi
report: The attacker exploited the _borrow function in Abracadabra's Cauldron contract, which used a stale price cache for collateral valuation. During the borrow operation, the protocol relied on an outdated exchange rate between xSUSHI and MIM that had not been updated despite a 15% market drop in xSUSHI price. This allowed the attacker to borrow more MIM than justified by current market value. Immediately after borrowing, the attacker triggered a price update to reflect the lower xSUSHI price, then liquidated their own position at the new rate—repaying less MIM while reclaiming all collateral. The core vulnerability lay in the _borrow function’s failure to ensure fresh oracle prices before committing to a loan, enabling arbitrage through price-cache manipulation.
